description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G1 Thamna

name: The Giza Necropolis
description: This complex of ancient monuments includes the three pyramid complexes known as the Great Pyramids, the massive sculpture known as the Great Sphinx, several cemeteries, a workers' village and an industrial complex.

name: The Gordian Palace
description: A large Roman Peristyle House with interior gardens, pools, and baths.

name: The Great Sphinx
description: A monumental limestone sphinx situated on the Giza plateau, likely connected with the reign of Pharaoh Khafre.

name: The Queen's Megaron (Palace of Minos)
description: The Queen's Megaron is a room in the Minoan "Palace of Minos" at Knossos. It includes the famous Dolphin fresco.

name: The Sassi and the Park of the Rupestrian Churches of Matera
description: A series of rock-cut dwellings and buildings.

name: Theater of Icarion Aegeidas (Attica)
description: A small deme theater south of the Agora.

name: Theater of Nero
description: The Theater of Nero located in the Vaticanus Ager is relatively little known and receives little attention in the ancient sources. Archaeological discoveries announced in the summer of 2023 seem to point to remains of the theater preserved beneath the courtyard of the Palazzo della Rovere located between the Borgo Santo Spirito and the Via della Conciliazione. The first-century CE theater seems to have been dismantled by the second-century CE.

name: Theatrum Marcelli
description: Theatrum Marcelli (Theater of Marcellus) is an ancient Roman theater in the Circus Flaminius. Augustus completed its construction and dedicated it in 12 BC.

name: Thebai/Thebae
description: The ancient city of Thebes in Boeotia (modern Greece).

name: Thera
description: An ancient settlement located atop the modern Messavouno mountain on the island that shared its name in antiquity (modern Thira/Santorini).

name: Thermi, Lesbos
description: Thermi, Lesbos, was a Bronze Age port-cum-settlement that flourished during the third millennium BCE.

name: Thesbe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C5 Thesbe

name: Thespiai
description: Thespiai was an ancient city of Boeotia, located at the foot of Mt. Helicon.

name: Tholey-Vareswald
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H3 Tholey-Vareswald

name: Tholos of Athens
description: The so-called "Tholos" located on the west side of the Athenian Agora was a circular civic building that took the place of archaic-period buildings associated with the Peisistratid tyrants. The Classical tholos served as a civic dining hall for the prytany council.

name: Throne Room in Palace of Minos
description: The Throne Room in the "Palace of Minos" at Knossos, a room with ritual features located on the west side of the central courtyard of the palace. The room takes its name from the masonry throne, flanked by frescoes depicting griffins, that occupies one of its walls.

name: Thugga/Tokai?
description: Thugga/Tokai? (Dougga) is an ancient site in Tunisia with a rich heritage that includes Numidian-Berber, Punic, ancient Roman, and Byzantine phases. The city's Roman phase is particularly well preserved and UNESCO listed the site as a World Heritage Site in 1997.

name: Thule (island)
description: An island mentioned frequently -- and often metaphorically -- in texts of the Roman period, said by Ptolemy, Strabo, Pliny, and others to lie to the north of Britannia. Some scholars associate it with the island known today as Shetland, but the geographic association even in antiquity (when it was more than just a rhetorical term) varied (see further Todd in New Pauly).

name: Thyateira
description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor, located at modern Akhisar in Turkey. Visible remains today are limited; they include the archaeological area of Tepe Mezarı in the center of the modern town.

name: Thyrreion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Thyrreion

name: Tigranocerta/Cholimma/Chlomaron
description: A city in Armenia, founded by Tigranes II as capital of his kingdom in the first half of the 1st century BC.

name: Til-Barsip Archive 1
description: In House C1 in the Lower City of Til-Barsip, 22 Assyrian clay tablets, as well as 2 Aramaic texts, were discovered in and around the doorway between Room XI and XII in a secondary position. The earliest text dates to 683 BC, during the reign of Sennacherib (704-681 BC), but most of the documents date later, to the reign of Ashurbanipal (668-ca. 631 BC). The archive belonged to a man called Hanni, with some of the texts also concerning a man named Ištar-duri, and consists of purchase and loan documents as well as some others, such as a juridicial agreement.

name: Tilmen Höyük
description: Tilmen Höyük is a tell located near Islahiye, Gaziantep province, Turkey. Human occupation in the fourth millennium BCE. Cuneiform inscriptions have been discovered at the site.

name: Tiscali
description: A cave settlement on Monte Tiscali with the remains of over 60 huts.

name: Tisna/Titne
description: An ancient Aeolian city, Tisna was built on a pair of volcanic hills northwest of modern Uzunhansanlar (Aliağa, İzmir, Turkey).

name: Tol-e Sangi (Fars)
description: Neolithic archaeological site in the Fars province of present-day Iran.

name: Toprakkale
description: A large, fortified Kushan urban center (first to third centuries).

name: Torone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B2 Torone

name: Torre degli Embrici
description: An imperial Roman villa with a bath complex that emerged in the first and second centuries AD, transitioning to a fortified site after the sixth century AD.

name: Torre del Filosofo
description: The so-called "Torre del Filosofo" was a structure on Mt. Etna that may have been built by the Roman emperor Hadrian in honor of the Greek philosopher Empedocles as local legend held that he once climbed the mountain and died by falling into the volcano.

name: Torre del Monumento
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C4 Torre del Monumento

name: Torrellafuda
description: Torrellafuda is the site of a Talayotic settlement on the island of Minorca.

name: Torretrencada
description: Torretrencada is a Talayotic settlement of the early first millennium BCE.

name: Torretta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Torretta

name: Toshbuloq
description: Toshbuloq or Tashbulak was a high-altitude urban center located along the medieval Silk Route (6th–11th century CE).

name: Toumba Apsalou (Pella)
description: A partially excavated ancient settlement at Toumba Apsalou, northwest of Apsalos, Pella.

name: Tourraque d'Ortolas
description: A Gallo-Roman tower.

name: Tower of the Winds
description: Also known as the Horologion of Andronikos Kyrrhestes, the Tower of the Winds is an octagonal Pentelic marble clocktower located in the "Roman" Agora that functioned as a horologion.

name: Tragilos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 B3 Tragilos

name: Tratturo Caniò
description: Tratturo Caniò is an archaeological site located in the territory of the ancient city of Setia in the Pontine Plain of Lazio, Italy. An archaic temple sacred to Iuno is known at the site, and archaeological prospective in 2009 revealed that there are also earlier Bronze and Iron Age remains at the site.

name: Trebbio
description: An Iron Age settlement covering some 20 ha was identified at Trebbio, located to the south of Sansepolcro.

name: Tremona
description: A multi-phase settlement - Neolithic to Medieval periods - that is now preserved as an archaeological site.

name: Tres (islands)
description: A group of small islands located in the Alboran Sea off the coast of Morocco.

name: Tres Tabernae
description: A statio at the point where the Via Appia crosses the Via Ninfina.

name: Triconch Palace at Butrint
description: The so-called Triconch Palace.

name: Trileukoi (islands)
description: Small, rocky islets in Galicia located adjacent to Cabo Ortegal.

name: Trimithis
description: An ancient settlement of at least Roman and Late Antique date, situated in the Dahkleh Oasis at a site now called Amheida.

name: Triumphal Arch of Glanum
description: A Roman triumphal arch built in the early first century A.D.

name: Triumphal Arch of Orange
description: A triumphal arch most likely built during the reign of Augustus to honor veterans of the Gallic Wars and Legio II Augusta.

name: Tropaeum Augusti/Alpium Tropaeum/Alpis Summa/Alpis Maritima
description: A victory monument built by Augustus (5 B.C.) to commemorate the subjugation of the tribes of the western Alps. The Romans considered the spot to be "In Alpe Maritima".

name: Tropaeum Traiani (monument)
description: A war memorial near the ancient settlement of Tropaeum Traiani (modern Adamclisi)

name: Troulli tis Korfis
description: A hilltop sanctuary, in use from the Bronze Age until the time of the Roman Empire.

name: Trypitos
description: A small Hellenistic town excavated on the Trypitos promontory E of Siteia, Lasithi Crete. It is probably to be identified with Polichna, attested in ancient literary sources.

name: Tserli
description: The foundations of a Hellenistic farm located near the shores of Lake Boibe in Thessaly.

name: Tsimintiri Archaic Site
description: The small Aegean island of Tsimintiri, located between Despotiko and Antiparos, has produced archaeological evidence for a large sanctuary and/or other public complex of archaic date, likely to be associated with the extensive archaic sanctuary of Mandra, located on the island of Despotiko, to which Tsimintiri was connected in antiquity.

name: Tsoungiza (Archaia Nemea)
description: The archaeological site of Tsoungiza shows evidence of human activity since the Early Neolithic period. Modern excavations began under Carl Blegen in 1924.

name: Tubusuctu
description: An ancient settlement located in the modern Soummam river valley at a place called Tiklat, which is near modern El Kseur in Algeria's Kabylia province. Here the Roman emperor Augustus established a veteran colony. Bishops of Tubusuctu are attested in the fifth century CE.

name: Tugunbulak
description: Tugunbulak was a Medieval city on the Silk Route covering an area of ca. 120 hectares (300 acres).

name: Tugurium Romuli
description: Ancient textual sources, including Dionysius of Halicarnassus and Plutarch, place the purported dwelling place of Romulus near the Scalae Caci at the southwestern corner of the Palatine Hill in Rome. Whether the place cited by ancient texts and maintained in antiquity until at least the fourth century CE corresponds to the archaeological remains of archaic huts excavated near the Clivus Victoriae remains a matter of debate.

name: Tul-i Ājuri
description: Mound where glazed brick has been found, currently overbuilt.

name: Tulul Abu Jawan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 91 F4 Tulul Abu Jawan

name: Tulul al-Baqarat
description: Tulul al-Baqarat is an archaeological site located seven kilometers northeast of Tell al-Wilayah, Iraq. The settlement was occupied from the fourth millennium BCE to the Islamic period. It may have been the Early Dynastic city of Kesh.

name: Tulūl al-Fāj
description: Two mounds from Parthian and Sassanian periods where Old Babylonian bricks have been found.

name: Tulūl al-Hummar
description: Mound where stamped bricks have been found.

name: Tulūl Bismāyah
description: Group of kassite mounds, some of which have been completely obliterated or covered by residential buildings.

name: Tulūl Yassir
description: Mound and find place for cuneiform inscription.

name: Tumbus (Nubia)
description: An archaeological site in northern Sudan that includes Tombos Island and the adjacent riverbank. The site is located at the Third Cataract of the Nile River and was occupied form the mid-18th Dynasty of Egypt and through the 25th Dynasty.

name: Turris Libisonis
description: A settlement of northwestern Sardinia, located at the mouth of the Mannu River. The site served as a port in Phoenician, Carthaginian, and Roman phases of occupation. The modern Porto Torres stands on the site, about 10 miles distant from Sassari.

name: Turrita
description: Now unlocated, Turrita was a station (statio) near Pisae.

name: Tuscan Archipelago
description: A chain of islands between the Ligurian Sea and Tyrrhenian Sea.

name: Tyana/Col. Aurelia Antoniniana
description: An ancient city of Cappadocia that was the capital of the Neo-Hittite kingdom in the first millennium B.C.

name: Typaneai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B2 Typaneai

name: Tzoungiza
description: A Middle Helladic settlement excavated near Nemea.

name: Uchi Maius
description: Uchi Maius was a township of Africa Proconsularis where Marius settled veterans. It did not achieve the status of colonia until the third century.

name: Uffington White Horse
description: The Uffington White Horse is stylized prehistoric hill figure dating to the later prehistoric period in England.

name: Uley
description: At West Hill, Uley, Gloucestershire, excavations revealed the remains of a multi-period ritual site. This site remained in use from the Neolithic period to the Middle Ages. An Iron Age sanctuary was replaced by a Romano-British temple dedicated to Mercury in the early second century A.D.

name: Ulucak Höyük
description: An ancient settlement mound located 25 km west of İzmir with occupation ranging from the Early Neolithic to Late Roman and Early Byzantine periods.

name: Umbilicus urbis Romae
description: The remains of a monument marking the symbolic navel of the city of Rome that dates to the reign of Septimius Severus.

name: Umm al-Ḥafryāt
description: Mound, find place for inscribed brick.

name: Umm al-Wawiya
description: Umm al-Wawiya is an archaeological site in present-day Iraq. It is located in the vicinity of the ancient site of Uruk, and some scholars have argued that Umm al-Wawiya could be the site of the ancient town of Durum.

name: Umm el-Marra
description: Bronze age city in northern Syria, located approximately 50 kilometers east of Aleppo.

name: Umm Towat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 78 C3 Umm Towat

name: Umma
description: Umma was an important ancient city of Sumer but has not been scientifically excavated. The mainstream of scholarly opinion associates the ancient site with Tell Jokha, Iraq, located in Iraq's Northwestern Dhi Qar Governorate. This area is remote and is associated with a network of ancient canals. Tell Jokha measures about 1,500 meters in diameter.

name: Unexplored Mansion at Knossos
description: While Arthur Evans gave this archaeological site the moniker "Unexplored mansion", it was in fact an annexe of the "Little Palace" and post-dates it. The "Unexplored Mansion" was built in MM IIIB or LM IA.

name: Unfinished obelisk
description: Although never completed, the 'unfinished obelisk' at Aswan in Egypt is the largest known ancient obelisk.

name: Unit IV-1 at Nichoria
description: Unit IV-1 is a large, megaron-style building constructed during the 11th century BCE at the site of Nichoria in Messenia, Greece.

name: Unnamed archaeological site
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 K2 no. 75 (Opuk UKR)

name: Unnamed road
description: An ancient road linking Nomentum and Eretum. Portions of the road are extant, particularly within an archaeological area at Monterotondo.

name: Unnamed rock relief
description: An unnamed Persian rock relief located near Sundurlu.

name: Unnamed settlement and temple
description: An unnamed Gallo-Roman settlement and temple, located near modern Champallement-Compierre in France.

name: Arch at Zama Regia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D4 unnamed unlabeled ((7 monuments, 1 cemetery, 2 walls))

name: Arch (36.54, 9.98)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E3 unnamed unlabeled ((15 monuments))

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E3 unnamed unlabeled ((15 monuments))

name: Arch N of Sidi-Ali-Bou-Khraled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 C1 unnamed unlabeled ((5 monuments, 2 cemeteries))

name: Ur(i)
description: A major Sumerian city-state founded during the Ubaid period, ca. 3800 B.C. In archaeological terms, Ur is particularly well known for the lavish tombs of its Royal Cemetery, its massive Ziggurat. The site served as the capital of the Ur III dynasty.

name: Uragh Stone Circle
description: A stone circle measuring 33 by 26 feet with 11 standing stones located near Gleninchaquin Park, Tuosist, County Kerry, Ireland.

name: Urakka
description: A town mentioned in Neo-Assyrian sources that is likely to be identified as Tell ʿĀmūdā.

name: Uronarti
description: An ancient settlement located on an island in the Nile just south of the Second Cataract, now in the north of Sudan. An ancient fortress dating to the reigns of the Middle Kingdom pharaohs (19th century BC), Senusret I and Senusret III, is found there.

name: Uruk/Orchoe/Erech/Orikut
description: Uruk was an ancient Sumerian (and later Babylonian) city located on the Euphrates river. At its height (ca. 2900 BC), Uruk's population may have topped 50,000 people and its walls enclosed an area of more than six square kilometers, making it the largest city in the world at that point in time.

name: Uşaklı Höyük
description: A Bronze and Iron Age site in Yozgat province, Turkey, tentatively identified as the Hittite town of Zippalanda (Çadır Höyük is another candidate for the location of Zippalanda).

name: Utica
description: A Punic center located west of Carthage.

name: Uushigiin Uver
description: A Bronze Age archaeological site with Deer Stones located at Uushigiin Ovor (UO), Mongolia, that is included in the cultural landscape "Deer Stone Monuments and Related Bronze Age Sites", inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2023.

name: Valcamonica
description: Located in the Lombardy plain, the Valcamonica assemblage constitutes one of the world's greatest collections of prehistoric petroglyphs – created over a period of 8,000 years. The site was enlisted by UNESCO in 1971.

name: Valetium/Balesium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 H3 Valetium/Balesium

name: Valle dei Templi
description: The "Valle dei Templi" of Agrigento is an archaeological site in Sicily located on a ridge, as opposed to the false suggestion of the conventional name, outside modern Agrigento.

name: Van Fortress
description: Van Fortress is an ancient citadel of Urartu built during the ninth through seventh centuries BC.

name: Vani
description: Vani (7th -1st centuries BCE) is an ancient city located in the Colchis Lowlands on the western bank of the Sulori River.

name: Vasio
description: Vasio (modern Vaison-la-Romaine) was the civitas capital of the Vocontii and eventually received Roman status by the late first century BC.

name: Vatican Obelisk
description: An obelisk from Heliopolis in Egypt that the emperor Caius erected in the Circus Gaii et Neronis. It now stands in St. Peter's square in Vatican City, having been re-erected there by Domenico Fontana in 1586.

name: Veh Ardashir/Coche/Mahoza/‘Seleucia’
description: A significant Hellenistic city that served as a capital of both the Seleucid and Parthian empires. The city was founded by Seleucus Nicator.

name: Veliki Preslav
description: Veliki Preslav was a fortified site that served as the capital of the Tsar Simeon I of the Bulgarian Empire. The site flourished during the ninth and tenth centuries CE. The site includes both 'inner' and 'outer' fortified areas.

name: Veneticae (islands)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 B1 Veneticae Inss.

name: Ventimiglia Roman Theater
description: A Roman theater at modern Ventimiglia in Italy, dating to the early second century CE.

name: Vessagiriya
description: Vessagiriya is the site of an ancient Buddhist forest monastery in Sri Lanka. It is considered part of the ruined site of Anuradhapura. The monastery was initially built in the middle of the third century BCE and was expanded in the late fifth century CE. A Roman bronze coin, as well as punch-marked coins and/or ingots, were excavated at Vessagiriya in the first decade of the 20th century.

name: Vicus Martis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 D3 Vicus Martis

name: Villa delle Guardiole
description: A Late Republican to early Imperial coastal villa located at the mouth of the Fosso delle Guardiole near Castrum Novum. Two artificial fish ponds are associated with the villa.

name: Villa Imperiale of Milan
description: A palatial Roman villa dating to the reign of Maximian.

name: Vlithias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 A2 Vlithias

name: Volcei
description: Volcei (modern Buccino) was an ancient city of Lucania that was subjugated by the Romans in 209 BC.

name: Volsinii
description: Volsinii, also referred to as Volsinii Novi, is thought to be the settlement where Rome resettled the survivors of the Volsinian rebellion of 264 BCE. This episode is discussed by Zonaras (8. 7. 8). A late republican center at Bolsena, Italy, with civic architecture (forum) and domus has been excavated since 1946 by the École Française and is identified as Volsinii Novi.

name: Volubilis
description: A town of Mauretania Tingitana and a Roman colony.

name: Vounari
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Vounari

name: Vroulia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 F4 Vroulia

name: Vryokastro
description: A Bronze Age settlement located on the south coast of the island of Tinos to the west of Agios Phokas.

name: Wadi es-Saba Monuments
description: Two inscribed reliefs of a Neo-Babylonian king (probably Nebuchadnezzar II) are carved on a rock face in the Wadi es-Saba (“Valley of the Lion”), southwest of Akroum, Lebanon.

name: Wall of Media
description: The Wall of Media, also known as Mēdías teîchos or the Wall of Semiramis, at Habl as-Sahr is mentioned by Xenophon in the Anabasis as a defensive ditch at the Euphrates built on the orders of Artaxerxes in the fifth century BC.

name: Welzheim: Ostkastell
description: One of two Roman forts located at modern Welzheim in Germany, the excavated remains of the "Ostkastell" are largely preserved. The west gate and a portion of the associated defensive wall was reconstructed in the 1970s.

name: West House at Vasiliki
description: The so-called West House is an architectural complex built as an annex to a larger building, the so-called Red House, in an Early Minoan settlement at Vasiliki on Crete.

name: Western gate of Serdica
description: Fortification at the western edge of the settlement of Serdica, constructed in the 2nd century CE and disused by the end of the 6th century CE. The site was excavated in 2011-2013 and is now an archaeological park.

name: Wilson's Arch
description: Stone archway constructed as part of the expansion of the Temple Mount in Jerusalem during the early Roman period.

name: Woodhenge
description: Woodhenge is the site of a "Neolithic Class II henge and timber circle monument" located within the Stonehenge UNESCO World Heritage Site in Wiltshire, England. It is located approximately 2 miles (3.2 km) northeast of Stonehenge (Durrington parish).

name: Workmen's rest-houses
description: Facilities built by the workers of the village of Deir el-Medina to serve as a place of rest and worship on the road linking the village of Deir el-Medina to the Valley of the Kings.

name: Xanthos
description: An ancient city of Lycia.

name: Xenagori (islands)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 B5 Xenagori Inss.

name: Xocalı
description: Extensive groups of Iron Age tumuli in the Lesser Caucasian mountains. A Neo-Assyrian seal bearing the name of Adad-nirari II (911-891 BCE) was found here during excavations in 1895.

name: Yaat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B5 Yaat

name: Yacimiento arqueologico de Doña Blanca
description: An archaeological site with multiple settlement horizons.

name: Yaoshan Site
description: The Yaoshan Site is part of the larger UNESCO cultural heritage landscape, "Archaeological Ruins of Liangzhu City". According to UNESCO, the Yaoshan Site dates ca. 3300-3100 BCE and covers an area of some 66.56 hectares (164.5 acres).

name: Yarim Tepe (Iran)
description: Yarim Tepe was the site of a Neolithic settlement located in Iran's Gorgan plain. Archaeological fieldwork was first undertaken by V. E. Crawford in 1963.

name: Yarim Tepe (Iraq)
description: Yarim Tepe (Iraq) is an archaeological site in Iraq's Sinjar Valley that emerged ca. 6000 BCE. Its later phases are associated first with the Hassuna culture, and then the Halaf and Ubaid cultures.

name: Yassi Höyük
description: Mound and find place for cuneiform inscription.

name: Yassıhöyük
description: A mound site in Kırşehir province in Turkey containing a palace or temple structure dated to the first half of the 2nd millennium BCE or the first half of the 1st millennium BCE.

name: Yazılıkaya (Eskişehir)
description: Yazılıkaya (also known as 'Midas City') is an archaeological site in Eskişehir Province, Turkey, known for its Phrygian rock-cut monuments, facades, altars, and niches dedicated to the Mother Goddess Matar.

name: Yedikilise
description: Destroyed Armenian monastery in the village of Bakraçlı where Urartian inscriptions have been found.

name: Yoncalı
description: Yoncalı is located near the modern cities of Malazgirt and Bulanık, at the western end of the Bulanık-Malazgirt plain. The Assyrian king Tiglat-pileser I placed an inscribed, rock-cut relief here to commemorate his victories in the region.

name: Yukarı Anzaf
description: Site for Urartian palace and citadel.

name: Yurta-Stroyno
description: Rural settlement of the Roman and Late Antique periods in Thrace, located near the modern village of Stroyno, Bulgaria.

name: Zabala
description: This Sumerian/Babylonian city (also known as Zabalam, now as modern Tell Ibzeikh) was a cult centre of the goddess Inanna/Ishtar. It was located near the Iturungal and Ninagina canals.

name: Zakros villa
description: A Minoan "villa" along the road leading south from Zakros, Lasithi, Crete.

name: Zakythos/Zacynthus (settlement)
description: An ancient settlement on the Aegean island of the same name.

name: Zaldapa
description: Zaldapa, located near Abrit, Bulgaria, was a fortified Late Roman settlement in Moesia Inferior. It was restored under Justinian I.

name: Zama Regia/Zama *Maior
description: Zama Regia/Zama *Maior in Africa Proconsularis was the site of the Battle of Zama on October 19, 202 B.C., at the close of the Second Punic War fought between Rome and Carthage.

name: Zaralulu
description: Evidence for second millennium B.C. copper working were discovered at the site.

name: Zawiyah
description: Zāwiyah (also Zawiyeh) is a site in the middle Euphrates region in what was part of the ancient kingdom of Suhu. The city was situated on the north bank of the Euphrates, a few kilometers west of Sur Jureh and Gleieh. Like many towns and cities of the ancient kingdom of Suhu, Zāwiyah is now underwater; the area was flooded after the construction of the Haditha dam. The city's ancient name is not known.

name: Zela
description: Zela (modern Zile, Turkey) was an important ancient city of north-central Asia Minor; Strabo claimed Semiramis had founded the site. Iulius Caesar prevailed in the Battle of Zela against Pharnaces II of The Kingdom of Pontus, coining the phrase "veni, vidi, vici".

name: Zenobiou (islands)
description: The Khuriya Muriya Islands are a group of five islands in the Arabian Sea.

name: Zernaki tepe
description: An ancient place in Van province, Turkey. It had a street grid, fortifications of mud brick on a polygonal masonry foundation, and at least 14 building inscriptions in Aramaic from the Parthian period. Professor Rafet Çavuşoğlu began a program of excavations in 2021, which appears to be unpublished as of October 2025.

name: Zeugma
description: An ancient settlement whose site is located in the Gaziantep province of modern Turkey, at the modern village of Belkis (a.k.a. Kavunlu). The majority of the ancient remains were covered by water in the year 2000, due to the construction of the Birecik Dam.

name: Ziwiye
description: Ziwiye is the name of a modern village in northwestern Iran. It is associated with an archaeological site from the 7th century BCE that was looted in the mid-20th century and subsequently partially excavated in a more scientific manner. The site's ancient name is not known.

name: Ziyaret Tepe
description: Mound and find place for cuneiform inscription.

name: Zominthos
description: A Minoan settlement dating from ca. 1800 BC onwards, Zominthos is well known for a large Minoan building discovered in 1982.

name: Zone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 F3 Zone

name: Zvartnots Cathedral
description: A seventh century AD church built by Catholicos Nerses the Builder (AD 641-653) and located in the Armavir Province of Armenia. The ruins of the church were listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: 'Ain el-Sabil Church
description: Late antique mud-brick church located at Ain el-Sabil in Egypt. The exact dating of the site is still unknown.

name: 'Evron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 'Evron

name: ‘Trebula’
description: A pre-Roman site in the valley of the Sangro river that was later converted to a Christian church.

name: "Basilica" of San Vitale
description: A church (but not a basilica) in Ravenna, Italy, established by Bishop Ecclesius in 526 AD and consecrated ca. 547 AD.

name: "The Great Basilica" at Butrint
description: The so-called "Great Basilica" is an early Christian basilica dating to the sixth century.

name: [Hagios Prokopios]
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 63 F3 [Hagios Prokopios]

name: *Chrononum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 *Chrononum

name: *Iciacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 *Iciacum

name: *Manatum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H2 *Manatum

name: *Marciacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 *Marciacum

name: *Maroialum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 *Maroialum

name: *Melita
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H3 *Melita

name: *Novivicum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F1 *Novivicum

name: *Pauliacum, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G2 *Pauliacum, Mon.

name: *Porsulae/Maximianopolis
description: *Porsulae/Maximianopolis, also known as Mosynopolis, was a Byzantine town along the Via Egnatia in Thrace; its position is close to the modern Greek city of Komotini.

name: *Qubba
description: A monastery constructed in the fifth century CE on the site of a cave previously inhabited by St. Paul the Anchorite.

name: *Randanum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 *Randanum

name: *Siroialum?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F3 *Siroialum?

name: *Vanisnesi
description: Extensive Roman ruins identified by Gsell (AAA, whence BAtlas) at a site he called Oum Sekak in the Hassenaoua district of modern Algeria. He estimates the site extent at 13 hectares and reports the remains of a church. An inscription that seems to provide the name of the "genius loci" (Genius Vanisnesi) was found in the vicinity. The site is not clearly visible in twenty-first century aerial/satellite imagery and most of the modern toponymy Gsell provides does not appear in the open-source, digital gazetteers and map tools. The site may now lie under modern development in the area.

name: Abbazia di San Giovanni in Venere
description: A Christian monastic complex that is located in the municipality of Fossacesia, Italy. The site occupies a hilltop at 107 m above sea level on the Adriatic Sea coast. The basilica dates originally from the thirteenth century.

name: Abbazia di San Vincenzo al Volturno
description: An historic Benedictine monastery located in the Province of Isernia, near the source of the river Volturno.

name: Abbazia San Claudio al Chienti
description: The church of San Claudio al Chienti is a Romanesque church building located in the municipality of Corridonia in the Chienti valley. It was founded in the eleventh century.

name: Abbey of Monte Cassino
description: A multi-period hilltop settlement with evidence for ancient terrace construction that now supports an abbey of the Benedictine order.

name: Abbeyknockmoy abbey
description: A ruined Medieval Cistercian abbey founded in 1190.

name: Ad Herculem
description: An ancient station on the Roman road to Gades recorded in the Antonine Itinerary, ad Herculem must have been associated with the Herakleion on the southern end of the island of Cotinussa. Scholars differ on the precise location; their efforts are complicated by significant landscape change since antiquity.

name: Ag. Andreas (Siphnos)
description: A Classical-Hellenistic period settlement on/around the site of an earlier Mycenaean citadel on the island of Siphnos, now partially occupied by the church of Agios Andreas.

name: Ag. Dimitrios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 inset Ag. Dimitrios

name: Ag. Isidoros
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Ag. Isidoros

name: Ag. Lot
description: The Sanctuary of Agios Lot is located at the southeastern end of the Dead Sea.

name: Ag. Mavri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Ag. Mavri

name: Ag. Nikitas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 C4 Ag. Nikitas

name: Ag. Panagia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D2 Ag. Panagia

name: Ag. Sophia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Ag. Sophia

name: Ag. Stephanos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Ag. Stephanos

name: Ag. Thomas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Ag. Thomas

name: Ag. Trias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E1 Ag. Trias

name: Agia Dynamis
description: A sixteenth-century chapel in Athens built atop an ancient shrine of Herakles.

name: Ain el Aouad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 Ain el Aouad

name: Ain Nagila
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Ain Nagila

name: Akoimeton Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Akoimeton Mon.

name: al-Husn Church
description: A ruined and now largely dispersed fifth-century CE church on the outskirts of al-Bara, Syria.

name: Alexandros, Mon.
description: Monastery founded by  Alexander the Acoemete in 782 CE on the left bank of the Euphrates, near the modern village of Halawa.

name: Amaras
description: An ancient settlement and early Christian site, now occupied partly by an Armenian Apostolic monastery and its 18th century church.

name: Ampass early Christian Church
description: The high altar of the present, Gothic-era Pfarrkirche (parish church) of modern Ampass (near Insbruck in Austria) was built over a series of earlier Christian churches, the earliest of which appears to date to the fifth century CE, based on excavated remains of a reliquary vault dating back to that period.

name: Amphitheater of Durrës
description: The Roman amphitheater of Durrës dates to the reign of Trajan. A small chapel built inside the amphitheater has mosaics likely dating to the 6th-8th centuries CE.

name: Apa Abraham, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Apa Abraham, Mon.

name: Apa Apollo
description: A Coptic monastery founded ca. 385/390 located 80 km north of Asyut, near the village of Dashlout, Egypt.

name: Apa Apollo, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Apa Apollo, Mon.

name: Apa Apollo, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Apollo, Mon.

name: Apa Bane, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Apa Bane, Mon.

name: Apa Djoore, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Apa Djoore, Mon.

name: Apa Epiphanius, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Apa Epiphanius, Mon.

name: Apa Ioannes, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Apa Ioannes, Mon.

name: Apa Makrobios, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Makrobios, Mon.

name: Apa Palemon, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Apa Palemon, Mon.

name: Apa Psoi, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Apa Psoi, Mon.

name: Apa Psote, Mon./Amba Bisada
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F4 Apa Psote, Mon./Amba Bisada

name: Apa Senouthou (monastery)
description: The "White Monastery", located near modern Tahta and Sohag in Egypt.

name: Apa Souroutos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Souroutos, Mon.

name: Apollo Asgelatos (temple)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 B5 Apollo Asgelatos, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: Two early Christian basilicae were superimposed over the ancient sanctuary of Apollo Dalios.

name: Apostoli, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Apostoli, Mon.

name: Aqaba Church
description: A pre-Constantinian Christian church located at ancient Aila in Jordan.

name: Arachnaion (mountain)
description: A mountain of the Argolid with a higher western peak (Profitis Ilias, 1199 masl) and a lower eastern peak (Mavrovouni or Arna, 1139 masl). Material from the Mycenaean, Archaic, and Classical periods has been found on the western peak and identified with altars of Zeus and Hera described by Pausanias. Aeschylus mentions its use as a signal station.

name: Archaic Temple at Ai Lia
description: Badly damaged foundations of the peristyle of a large Doric temple of the 6th or early 5th c. BCE, the center of which is now occupied by the modern chapel of Profitis Ilias on a rise NE of ancient Kyreneia. This temple was probably destroyed during the earthquake of 373 BCE and replaced by a smaller temple to the west, built from its rubble. A monumental great altar has also been excavated immediately east of the temple.

name: Archbishop's Chapel, Ravenna
description: A private, early Christian oratory in Ravenna, Italy, erected by Peter II as archbishop in A.D. 495.

name: Arian Baptistry
description: Baptistry built by Theodoric the Great in the late fifth or early sixth century AD

name: Arteda Civitate
description: An early medieval settlement in Spain (9th to 13th centuries CE) listed in contemporary Latin documents. This settlement is now identified with a second phase of occupation of an archaeological site known as El Forau de la Tuta near modern Arieda in Zaragosa province where a previously unknown Roman settlement has also been discovered. In addition to archaeological finds in situ, spolia from both the Roman and medieval phases have been identified in the more recent Ermita de San Pedro.

name: Asabaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Asabaa

name: Ashqelon/Ascalon
description: Ashqelon/Ascalon was an ancient city in coastal Palestine, located midway between Azotus and Gaza. It became independent in 104 B.C. and remained the only free city in Palestine. Strabo refers to it, while Pliny calls it a free city (oppidum libera).

name: Aula Palatina
description: The Aula Palatina is a Roman Imperial Basilica of the emperor Constantine (German: Konstantinbasilika) located at Trier, Germany. It was built ca. A.D. 310 as part of a Roman palace complex.

name: Ayios Filon
description: A ruined fifth-century CE basilica on the Karpass peninsula of Cyprus.

name: Bağin
description: Village where fragments of a Urartian stele have been found (one in a church, one in a fortress).

name: Baldersdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Baldersdorf

name: Balsignano
description: A fortified Medieval site located in Puglia.

name: Baptistery of Butrint
description: The Baptistery of Butrint is a round structure, with a diameter of 14.5 meters. It was built in the sixth century A.D. and was excavated in May 1928 by Luigi Maria Ugolini.

name: Baptistry of Neon
description: An octagonal brick structure erected by Bishop Ursus (late fourth or early fifth century AD) as part of a Basilica (destroyed in 1734). Bishop Neon completed the baptistry at the end of the fifth century AD.

name: Baqirha
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Baqirha

name: Barlaamus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A2 Barlaamus

name: Basilica
description: A large structure near the northeast corner of the Athenian Agora, built early in the second century AD, which may have been a basilica and perhaps served as a court.

name: Basilica
description: An important civic building in Pompeii and an early example of the building type, built between ca. 120 and 78 B.C. (VIII, 1, 1).

name: Basilica Aemilia
description: A basilica located at the juncture of the Sacra Via and the Argiletum flanking the Forum Romanum that was initially built in 179 B.C.E.

name: Basilica and convent of S. Ippolito
description: An important paleochristian complex located in the area of Portus.

name: Basilica at Aspendos
description: The remains of a Roman Basilica at Aspendos.

name: Basilica at Saepinum
description: The Basilica at Saepinum is positioned at the intersection of the cardo and the decumanus.

name: Basilica Benedettina di S. Michele Arcangelo
description: An abbey church located in Capua on the western slopes of Monte Tifata.

name: Basílica Casa Herrera
description: The Casa Herrera Basilica is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida". The paleochristian basilica was built in either the late fifth or early sixth century CE.

name: Basilica Constantini
description: The Basilica Constantini, also referred to as Basilica Nova ("New Basilica"), Basilica of Maxentius or Basilica of Maxentius and Constantine, is a colossal fourth-century basilica located along the Sacra Via in Rome. It is considered the largest ancient structure in the valley of the Forum Romanum and is typically deemed the final Roman civic basilica built in the ancient city.

name: Basilica Damasiana
description: The basilica and oratory of Damasianus, as well as the martyr tombs of Simplicius, Faustinus, Beatrice, and Ruinianus.

name: Basilica Damous el-Karita
description: A Christian basilica of the Byzantine period located in Carthage.

name: Basílica de Santa Eulalia
description: The Basílica de Santa Eulalia is a paleochristian site that is included as part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida". The early Christian features were superimposed upon previous, preexisting Roman features.

name: Basílica de Son Peretó
description: A Paleochristian site dating to the fifth century A.D.

name: Basílica des Fornàs de Torelló
description: Basílica des Fornàs de Torelló, a sixth-century CE paleochristian basilica with mosaic pavements located in Mahon, Minorca.

name: Basilica di San Marco
description: The ruins of a Christian basilica located near the Via Ardeatina on the grounds of the Calixtian complex have been connected with the Basilica of Saint Mark. The site was discovered accidentally in 1991 and is associated with the Catacomb of Balbina.

name: Basilica di Sant'Agnese fuori le mura
description: Pope Honorius I commissions the Basilica of Saint Agnes in the seventh century, replacing the earlier Constantinian structure.

name: Basilica di Sant'Anastasia al Palatino
description: The Basilica of Sant'Anastasia al Palatino was founded ca. 325 CE on the slopes of the Palatine. It is now a minor basilica of the Roman Catholic church. It is the national church for the Peruvian community of Rome and the Indian community of the Syro-Malabar rite. The original basilica was adjacent to the Circus Maximus and came to be dedicated to Saint Anastasia of Sirmium (281-304 CE.), a Roman woman martyred under Diocletian. In the acts of the synod of 499, the church is listed as the titulus Anastasiae.

name: Basilica di Santa Balbina all'Aventino
description: The basilica of Santa Balbina is a paleo-Christian basilica in Rome, located on the "little Aventine".

name: Basilica di Santa Francesca Romana
description: The Basilica of Santa Francesca Romana, also known as Santa Maria Nova, dates originally from the ninth century when the basilica was erected on the site of a preexisting oratory during the papacy of Paul I.

name: Basilica di Santa Sinforosa
description: A minor Paleochristian basilica located in Guidonia Montecelio that was dedicated to the patron saint of Tivoli.

name: Basilica di Santo Stefano in via Latina
description: The now-ruined basilica of Santo Stefano in Via Latina was a fifth-century CE early Christian basilica. It is now located within the confines of the archaeological park of the Via Latina tombs.

name: Basilica Hilariana
description: A basilica erected on the Caelian Hill in Rome under the patronage of Manius Publicus Hilarus and connected to the cult of Cybele and Attis.

name: Basilica Manastirine
description: An important extra-mural Christian site at Solina. The site includes the basilica and the cemetery of Manastirine, associated with a Christian martyr.

name: Basilica Neptuni
description: A building in the Campus Martius that originally dates to ca. 25 BCE.

name: Basilica of Campanopetra
description: The ruins of a three-aisled basilica of the sixth century CE located at Salamis in Cyprus.

name: Basilica of Carsulae
description: The civic Basilica of Carsulae is located on the forum.

name: Basilica of Constantine (Ostia)
description: The presence of a basilica created with funds donated by Constantine I in region 5 of ancient Ostia was known from textual sources and then confirmed via geophysical prospection and, ultimately, excavation.

name: Basilica of Cresconius
description: The Basilica of Cresconius is a church with a nave-and-side-aisle plan located at Cuicul (Djémila), Numidia, Algeria.

name: Basilica of Hildeguns
description: A basilica dating to the time of the Vandals.

name: Basilica of Iomnium
description: The basilica of Iomnium (Tigzirt) likely dates to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Basilica of Iunius Bassus
description: The Basilica of Junius Bassus (basilica Iunii Bassi) was a civil basilica on the Esquiline Hill in Rome. Its remains were demolished in 1930 to make way for the Seminario Pontificio di Studi Orientali, in via Napoleone III, 3. Junius Bassus held the consulship in 331 CE. The basilica is particularly known for its opus sectile decorations. In the fifth century, the basilica was remodeled into the church of Sant'Andrea Catabarbara.

name: Basilica of Lechaion
description: The basilica of Lechaion was dedicated to Leonides, the bishop of Athens. It is one of the most important early Byzantine monuments in the Peloponnese.

name: Basilica of Lucus Feroniae
description: The Basilica of Lucus Feroniae communicates with the forum and is linked to the apsidal space identified as the Augusteum.

name: Basilica of Saint Augustine in Campo Marzio
description: The Basilica of Saint Augustine of Hippo in Camp Martius is a titular minor basilica of the Roman Catholic church located in the Campus Martius of Rome. The basilica was conceived in 1286 and the current basilica was completed in 1483.

name: Basilica of Saint-Pierre-aux-Nonnains
description: A Christian basilica that incorporates the remains of a fourth-century AD Roman gymnasium.

name: Basilica of San Clemente al Laterano
description: The eleventh-century Basilica of San Clemente al Laterano is a Roman Catholic basilica dedicated to Clement I. It sits atop a three-tiered series of architectural remains stretching back to the Republican period. The earliest phase is represented by the foundations of a building destroyed by the fire of A.D. 64.

name: Basilica of Sant'Apollinare in Classe
description: A basilica begun in the sixth century AD by Bishop Ursicinus with financial support from the Greek banker Iulianus Argentarius.

name: Basilica of Santa Maria in Trastevere
description: The Basilica di Santa Maria in Trastevere is a titular minor basilica located in the Trastevere district of Rome, Italy.

name: Basilica of Santa Pudenziana
description: A palaeochristian church dedicated to St. Pudentiana located in Rome and, perhaps, the earliest site of Christian worship in the city. The church is located along the Via Urbana, which follows the course of the ancient vicus Patricius.

name: Basilica of Septimius Severus
description: The Basilica of Septimius Severus belongs to the Severan Forum at Lepcis Magna.

name: Basilica of St. Achillios
description: The Basilica of St. Achillios at Larissa is an early Byzantine basilica dedicated to the city's patron saint, St. Achillios. The site was discovered and excavated in 1978.

name: Basilica of St. Crispinus
description: The fourth-century Basilica of St. Crispinus is one of the largest known in Africa.

name: Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi
description: A papal basilica begun ca. AD 1228.

name: Basilica of St. John
description: The Basilica of St. John the Evangelist is located in Ephesus on the Ayasuluk Hill. It was built between 548 and 565 CE.

name: Basilica of St. Titus
description: A Christian basilica of the mid-sixth to seventh centuries.

name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Paray-le-Monial)
description: 12th-century church in Paray-le-Monial, France. This site was a popular pilgrimage site and is an important example of Cluniac architecture.

name: Basilica of Thyrsos
description: A fifth or sixth century AD (Early Christian) triple-aisled basilica including mosaic floors.

name: Basilica Opimia
description: A civil basilica built in the Forum Romanum ca. 121 BCE by Lucius Opimius. This basilica was likely razed in 7 BCE.

name: Basilica Porcia
description: The Basilica Porcia is considered the first named civil basilica in Rome. The elder Cato is responsible for its construction in 184 BCE. It seems to have been a small structure that took the place of several earlier private atria located between the Clivus Argentarius and the Curia Hostilia. The Basilica Porcia was destroyed by fire in 52 BCE.

name: Basilica Ulpia
description: The Basilica Ulpia is located in the Forum of Trajan at Rome and derives its name from the emperor Trajan's gentilician nomenclature. The basilica was considered a marvel of ancient Roman architecture by observers, including the historian Ammianus Marcellinus.

name: Bau/Pboou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Bau/Pboou

name: Bell-Loc d’Aro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Bell-Loc d’Aro

name: Belmonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E3 Belmonte

name: Bertelles
description: A "fortified basilica" in Cyrenaica, probably dating to the 6th century CE and associated with nearby farmsteads and tombs, the latter dating to the Roman period, but reused in late antiquity.

name: Bethabara
description: Bethabara, in modern Jordan, is an ancient site connected with John the Baptist. Bethabara is also attested on the sixth century A.D. "Madaba Map".

name: Biclaro?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Biclaro?

name: Binbirkilise
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 C2 Binbirkilise

name: Bir Abu Swera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Bir Abu Swera

name: Bir Ftouha basilica
description: An Early Christian pilgrimage complex and baptistry at Carthage.

name: Bishop Genesius Church
description: The Bishop Genesius Church at Jerash (Jordan) may be the earliest Christian church on the site. The church was built in the early seventh century, reusing a preexisting Roman temple.

name: Bıçkıcı Kalesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 A4 Bıçkıcı Kalesi

name: Blatades Monastery
description: A fourteenth century monastery.

name: Bou Kisnaden
description: This site, discussed in scholarly literature in the 20th century, seems from satellite imagery to have been impinged or destroyed in the construction of the Rocade Tipaza/Autoroute Tipaza - Cherchel in the early years of the 21st century. It seems to have been a villa with a central courtyard and primary entrance facing east, with a row of rooms with arched openings overlooking the wadi to the north. Vats and presses, possibly for processing grapes for wine, were discovered. Anselmino hypothesized that another, unexcavated structure immediately to the east was a church.

name: Brampton Old Church
description: The Brampton Old Church stands on the site of a Roman fort located 13 km northeast of Carlisle and 0.8 km south of the Stanegate.

name: Breviglieri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Breviglieri

name: Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D1 Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia

name: Brittablo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H4 Brittablo

name: Cantobennum, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 Cantobennum, Mon.

name: Cappella di Sant'Antonino (Finale Ligure)
description: A twelfth-century chapel located near the Castrum Perticae. The site is located in the municipal territory of modern Finale Ligure in Italy's Ligurian Alps.

name: Castelfusano Paleochristian basilica
description: Situated along the via Severiana are the remains of an anonymous Paleochristian basilica that was in use between the second and the seventh centuries AD.

name: Castellnou de Basella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 Castellnou de Basella

name: Castle Acre Priory
description: A priory founded in 1089 by William de Warenne and consecrated sometime between 1146 and 1148. Home of the first Cluniac order in England.

name: Castrum Caetani
description: A Medieval fortification that incorporates and repurposes the Roman mausoleum of Caecilia Metella.

name: Cathedral of Santa Maria Assunta
description: The Cathedral of Santa Maria Assunta on the island of Torcello is a Roman Catholic basilica initially founded in 639. The second basilica was begun in 1008.

name: Cathedral of St. James, Jerusalem
description: A 12th-century Armenian church in Jerusalem, the principal church of the Armenian Patriarchate of Jerusalem.

name: Cathedral of the Holy Cross at Aghtamar
description: Armenian church built on the island of Aghtamar by King Gagik I Artsruni between 915-921 CE.

name: Çatıören Church
description: Byzantine church remains at Çatıören in Rough Cilicia

name: Cattedrale di Santa Maria di Amiternum
description: The ruins of the cathedral of the fifth century are located in an area of the archaeological site of Amiternum that was occupied in the second and third centuries CE.

name: Cave of Saint Peter
description: A cave in the outskirts of Antioch that was converted into a church.

name: Chafagi Aamer
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F3 Chafagi Aamer

name: Chalia
description: Theopompus mentions Chalia as a town of Boeotia. It is also attested epigraphically and may have been a member of the Boeotian league. Fossey locates the city in the vicinity of the church of Agios Minas in modern Drosia. Archaeological finds in the area indicate human activity from as early as the Bronze Age.

name: Chapelle Sainte-Agathe
description: The chapelle Sainte-Agathe was originally a Roman-period thermal complex that was converted to a Christian church by the seventh century.

name: Chariton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Chariton

name: Chiesa di Sant'Ilario a Port' Aurea
description: A sixth-, seventh-, and eighth-century Christian church located near the Port'Aurea of Benevento was built during the Longobard period. The church was built atop Imperial remains and was an apsidal hall.

name: Chiesa di Santa Pudenziana, Narni
description: The church of Santa Pudenziana near Narni, Italy, dates from the ninth to eleventh centuries CE and reuses spoliated architectural elements from a Roman villa.

name: Chiesa Ipogea di San Nicola
description: A poorly understood paleochristian site located on the grounds of the Inviolata park in the comune of Guidonia-Montecelio in the greater metropolitan area of Rome. The cave site may originally have functioned as a mithraeum.

name: Christian Basilica, Ostia
description: The so-called Christian Basilica, located in Regio III of ancient Ostia, was discovered by Guido Calza in 1939. The structure likely dates to the fourth and fifth centuries CE. The interpretation of the structure and its remains has proven difficult, resulting in numerous theories that are often at odds with one another. One possibility is that the complex functioned as a xenodochium (a guest house for pilgrims).

name: Christian House Church
description: Church adapted from a private house building in Dura-Europos.

name: Church and early Christian necropolis at Vaste
description: An early Christian complex that included a cult building with several construction phases and a vast cemetery.

name: Church at Shams el-Din
description: The church, well visible in Google Earth, is in the SW sector of the archaeological area

name: Church at Trimithis
description: A structure at the city of Trimithis (Amheida) in the Dakhleh Oasis, identified as a church and dated to the 4th century CE.

name: Church in Sidi Khiribech
description: Ruins of a church likely built around the fifth century AD, on the summit of the low hill of Sidi Khiribech in Berenike. The character of the building and its position has suggested to some archaeologists a defensive as well as a religious purpose.

name: Church of Mren
description: 7th-century Armenian church in the Kars region of Turkey. This church is now ruined, but still is the second-largest domed basilica from the 7th-century Caucasus still extant.

name: Church of Panagia Chalkeon
description: An eleventh-century A.D. Byzantine church in Thessaloniki.

name: Church of Prophet Elijah
description: A fourteenth-century AD church in Thessaloniki.

name: Church of Saint Alexander Nevsky, Jerusalem
description: The nineteenth century Russian Orthodox church preserves within it remains of Constantine's original fourth century church of the Holy Sepulchre.

name: Church of Saint Catherine, Thessaloniki
description: A late Byzantine church in Thessalonica.

name: Church of Saint Demetrius
description: A seventh century AD church dedicated to the patron saint of Thessalonica; the present basilica stands on the site of a precursor with phases beginning in the fourth century AD.

name: Church of Saint Eusebius (Ronciglione)
description: A paleochristian sanctuary located along the Via Francigena.

name: Church of Saint Gregory of Tigran
description: Armenian church in Ani, in modern Eastern Turkey, completed in 1215.

name: Church of Saint Martin at Moissac
description: A Christian church built, at least in part, atop the ruins of a Gallo-Roman building that include a hypocaust.

name: Church of Saint Panteleimon
description: The Church of Saint Panteleimon (Thessaloniki) is a late Byzantine church.

name: Church of Saint Sophia
description: The Church of Saint Sophia, also referred to as the Old Bishopric, is an Eastern Orthodox church located in Nesebar, eastern Bulgaria. The church originally dates to the sixth century.

name: Church of San Primitivo
description: The so-called Church of San Primitivo in Monte Compatri. The church's bell tower was converted to a defensive tower around 1200.

name: Church of Santa Fosca
description: The Church of Santa Fosca on the island of Torcello is a Romanesque church dating initially to the ninth century CE.

name: Church of St. Demetrios Katephores
description: A church in Athens, excavated and demolished between 1859 and 1861, which was built on top of a section of the post-Herulian wall incorporating several hundred inscriptions and sculptures, mostly from the Diogeneion gymnasium.

name: Church of St. Etchmiadzin at Soradir
description: Sixth-century Armenian church in the town of Soradir (modern Yanal, Turkey).

name: Church of St. George
description: One of a group of eleven rock-hewn monolithic churches located in Lalibela, a city in the Amhara Region of Ethiopia. The church likely dates to the twelfth or thirteenth century.

name: Church of St. Gião
description: A Paleochristian sanctuary in Portugal.

name: Church of St. Nicholas Orphanos
description: A fourteenth-century A.D. church in Thessalonica.

name: Church of St. Peter
description: A twelfth-century AD Christian church was built atop the remains of the Roman temple of Apollo.

name: Church of the Acheiropoietos
description: A fifth century A.D. Byzantine church.

name: Church of the Annunziatella
description: The church of the Annunziatella is a Catholic church located in Rome's Ardeatino district. It is situated between the via Ardeatina and via di Grotta Perfetta. The church was established in the thirteenth century, as an inscription of Pope Honorius III attests.

name: Church of the Archangels at Sige
description: Church in the village of Sige (modern Kumyaka) on the southern shore of the Sea of Marmara. Constructed under Constantine VI in 780, the structure was restored twice, in 1448 and 1819. It remained in continuous use until it was ultimately abandoned in 1922.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles
description: A fourteenth century church at Thessalonica.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles (Patmos)
description: 17th-century church behind the Monastery of Saint John the Theologian in Patmos.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles at Constantinople
description: Construction of a church dedicated to the Twelve Apostles at Constantinople began in 330 CE under Constantine the Great, and was completed during the reign of his son Constantius II (331-367). A larger church was established on the same site in 550 by Justinian I. The church was demolished by Mehmed II in 1462, and the Fatih Mosque was constructed on its site.

name: Church of the Nativity
description: A church that many claim marks the birthplace of Jesus Christ is located in Bethlehem, now in the State of Palestine in the West Bank. The original church built on the site was commissioned by Constantine I and dedicated in May 339 CE. The church was destroyed by fire, likely in 529, and replaced by Justinian I.

name: Church of the Saviour, Thessaloniki
description: The Church of the Saviour, Thessaloniki, is a fourteenth-century Byzantine church.

name: Civil Basilica at Aphrodisias
description: The Civil Basilica at Aphrodisias was a three-aisled public hall measuring approximately 145 by 30 meters. It opened off the southwest corner of the South Agora. It dates to the late first century CE.

name: Civita di Bagno
description: Civita di Bagno is the modern name for a small settlement that dates back at least to the Roman Late Republican period. Within the modern town, several key monuments are known, such as a podium temple (late 2nd c. BCE?), identified as that of Feronia by an inscription from the reign of Tiberius, a bath complex beneath Palazzo Oliva, and the medieval remains of the cathedral of San Massimo which reuses much ancient material, as well as funerary remains along the ancient road network. On the adjacent Moritola hill is a vast system of terracing, which in its earliest phase dates to the 2nd c. BCE, that supported a monumental complex of difficult interpretation, but with an important hydraulic infrastructure. This complex underwent multiple modifications over the centuries; in the 5th c. CE the complex was transformed by the installation of two kilns and a limekiln. By the 6th c. CE Civita di Bagno was the seat of a bishop.

name: Col. Ruscino
description: Col. Ruscino was an Iberian oppidum and town of Narbonese Gaul.

name: Corvey
description: The abbey was first founded in 815 by Charlemagne's cousins, Wala and Adelard.

name: Costa Bellene/Ballene (Capo Don?)
description: A Roman mansio on the via Iulia Augusta. An Early Christian complex (basilica, baptistery, cemetery) developed nearby and lasted until the Middle Ages.

name: Crypt and church of San Lussorio
description: The Romanesque church of San Lussorio dates to the early twelfth century.

name: Cyriacus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Cyriacus, Mon.

name: Daphni Monastery
description: The current Daphni Monastery in Attica dates to the eleventh century, yet its earlier Christian phases belong to the sixth century AD when a Christian site grew atop the abandoned and destroyed sanctuary of Apollo Daphnaios.

name: Deir Abou Makar
description: A Coptic Orthodox monastery located in the Wadi El Natrun northwest of Cairo.

name: Deir Abu Fam
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Abu Fam

name: Deir Abu Harmina
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Abu Harmina

name: Deir Abu Hennes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Abu Hennes

name: Deir Abu Masis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F4 Deir Abu Masis

name: Deir Abu Matta
description: Late antique settlement, possibly monastic, with substantial remains of a church, mud-brick structures, and tombs.

name: Deir Am-Nasara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Am-Nasara

name: Deir Amba Baramos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 C4 Deir Amba Baramos

name: Deir Amba Bishoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 C4 Deir Amba Bishoi

name: Deir Anba Bakhum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Anba Bakhum

name: Deir Anba Pschoi/Deir Anba Bishai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Anba Pschoi/Deir Anba Bishai

name: Deir Antush
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Antush

name: Deir Dronkeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir Dronkeh

name: Deir el Qal'a
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G1 Deir el Qal'a

name: Deir el-Adra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir el-Adra

name: Deir el-Bakhit
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Deir el-Bakhit

name: Deir el-Dik
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir el-Dik

name: Deir el-Kosseir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D2 Deir el-Kosseir

name: Deir el-Malek
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Deir el-Malek

name: Deir el-Muttin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir el-Muttin

name: Deir el-Qattal el-Bizanti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 71 A3 Deir el-Qattal el-Bizanti

name: Deir el-Shuhada
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir el-Shuhada

name: Deir el-Sourian
description: A monastery located in the modern Wadi El Natrun in Egypt, likely founded in the 6th century CE. The ancient church and some of its early decorative program survive.

name: Deir Madoud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Madoud

name: Deir Magara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir Magara

name: Deir Rumhan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Rumhan

name: Deir Sombat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Sombat

name: Deir Umm Arad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Umm Arad

name: Deir Umm Butme
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Umm Butme

name: Deirouné
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of a monastic chapel. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Dermech basilica
description: A Christian basilica of Byzantine Carthage.

name: Domus Cilonis
description: The house of Lucius Fabius Cilo, praefectus urbi in 203, consul in 193 and 204, and a close friend of the emperor Septimius Severus. The house was a gift of the Severans and appears on the Severan marble plan. Its site is now occupied by the basilica of Santa Balbina on the "little Aventine".

name: Doss Trento
description: Doss Trento is a small hill that lies along the course of the Adige River. A Paleochristian basilica was located on the hillside.

name: Dranda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Dranda

name: ed-Deir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 ed-Deir

name: Église Saint-Vincent de Marimbault
description: A 12th-century CE church thought to have been built on the site of a Gallo-Roman villa. It is located in the modern French commune of Marimbault (Gironde).

name: El Bovalar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 El Bovalar

name: el-Arba'in
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 el-Arba'in

name: el-Fra'iyeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Fra'iyeh

name: el-Khirbet
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Khirbet

name: el-Ma'yen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Ma'yen

name: Epaone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D3 Epaone

name: Episcopal complex of Eio-el Tolmo de Minateda (Héllín, Albacete, Spain)
description: The episcopal complex of Eio at El Tolmo de Minateda dates to the terminal sixth century CE and was in use during the following two centuries. Its construction may have been directed by Visigothic officials in Toletum.

name: Erebe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Erebe

name: Eremo di San Michele (Coli)
description: The cavern of San Michele was a hermit's dwelling place that is located near a cavern known as the "cave of San Colombano". This hermitage lies in a relatively solitary area of the valley of the Curiasca di San Michele stream, a tributary of the Trebbia river (Italian municipality of Coli, province of Piacenza).

name: Eremo di San Michele Arcangelo
description: A rock-cut Christian sanctuary located on Monte Altino in the Monti Aurunci that was begun ca. A.D. 830.

name: Etchmiadzin Cathedral
description: A fourth century A.D. church located in Ejmiatsin, Armenia. This is the central cathedral of the Mother See of Holy Etchmiadzin of the Armenian Apostolic Church and was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: Euphrasian Basilica
description: An Early Byzantine Christian basilica located in Poreč, Croatia.

name: Faras Cathedral
description: The cathedral in Faras, founded in 620 and rebuilt in the 8th and 10th centuries, respectively, was the seat of the Diocese of Faras. The site had well-preserved wall paintings from the 8th to 9th centuries. The cathedral was flooded by the creation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s.

name: Farfa Abbey
description: A Benedictine abbey located ca. 60 km from Rome.

name: Farsh el-Qasr
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 Farsh el-Qasr

name: Farsh Habash
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Farsh Habash

name: Forum and basilica at Venta
description: The forum-basilica at Venta date to the first and second centuries A.D. and are of the so-called "principia" type. The basilica measures ca. 54.6 x 20.1 m and was of the Corinthian order.

name: Forum Cassii
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 C4 Forum Cassii

name: Furfo
description: A vicus of the Vestini located in the upper Aterno valley in Abruzzo. The ancient settlement was located some 15 km south of modern L'Aquila, in the vicinity of S. Maria di Forfona near Barisciano. Furfo lay on the Via Claudia Nova between Amiternum and Peltuinum.

name: Gallarus Oratory
description: An early Christian church in Ireland, originally built between the sixth and the ninth centuries.

name: Gallo-Roman Structure at Moissac
description: A Gallo-Roman structure including hypocaust and other remains of a bath, located beneath the later Church of Saint Martin in modern Moissac, France. The bath may have belonged to a villa of the fourth-fifth centuries CE.

name: Gandzasar
description: 13th century Armenian cathedral located near the village of Vank in the disputed region of Nagorno-Karabakh/Artsakh.

name: Gantiadi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 F1 Gantiadi

name: Garizein (mountain)
description: One of two mountains in the immediate vicinity of the West Bank city of Nablus.

name: Gasr el-Gatres
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B1 Gasr el-Gatres

name: Gasr Silu
description: Gasr Silu, Libya, is an ancient site documented by the British School at Rome under John Bryan Ward-Perkins in 1969. Field study was conducted by Sheila Gibson and Margaret Browne. Photographs in the BSR archive document architectural remains at the site that include productive installations and the remains of two Byzantine churches. A Byzantine fort (gasr) was also identified.

name: Gasr Stablous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 13 (Gasr Stablous)

name: Gastaldaga area and the Episcopal complex
description: A Longobard complex in Cividale del Friuli.

name: Gebel Tafnis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Gebel Tafnis

name: Gebel Teir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D2 Gebel Teir

name: Gerasimos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 H2 Gerasimos

name: Germeshin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Germeshin

name: Geth Rabbi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 Geth Rabbi

name: Glak, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Glak, Mon.

name: Golyamo Belovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Golyamo Belovo

name: Great Church of Hippo Regius
description: The Great Church of Hippo Regius is located in the Christian quarter of Hippo. The church is associated with Saint Augustine, who was bishop in Hippo from 395 until he died in 430 CE.

name: Hagia Sophia
description: A major paleochristian, then Byzantine, church in Istanbul; subsequently an Islamic mosque, and today a museum.

name: Hagia Sophia, Iznik
description: Hagia Sophia, Iznik, Turkey, was founded as a Christian basilica in the fourth century CE. Justinian I rebuilt the site in the mid-6th century CE. An earthquake in the eleventh century destroyed that church, and it was then substantially rebuilt following 1065. It is to that rebuilding that much of the present structure pertains. Following the Ottoman conquest, the church became a mosque in 1331. The site served that function until ca. 1935, when it became a museum. Following 2011, it again served as a mosque.

name: Hagia Sophia, Thessaloniki
description: A church to Saint Sophia dating originally from the third century A.D.

name: Hasanaliler Basilica
description: Hasanaliler Church is a 6th-century Byzantine church.

name: Hephaisteion
description: The temple of Hephaistos located on the northwest side of the Agora of Athens dates to the middle of the fifth century B.C.

name: Horeb Mons, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Horeb Mons, Mon.

name: Horvat 'Erav
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B3 Horvat 'Erav

name: Hosios Loukas
description: A tenth-century walled monastery in Boeotia, Greece.

name: Hyrkania/Kastellion
description: Hyrkania was an ancient fortress in the Judaean Dessert that was rebuilt as the Byzantine monastery of Kastellion (ca. A.D 492).

name: I Cappuccini
description: The church and convent "I Cappuccini" in Frascati was the site of a Roman villa.

name: Ilissos Basilica
description: An early Christian basilica on the Ilissos River in Athens that may date to the fifth century.

name: Ilıca
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Ilıca

name: Imst
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Imst

name: Iohannes Urtius, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Iohannes Urtius, Mon.

name: Ishkhani (monastery)
description: A church and monastery in Tao-Klarjeti, first constructed in the 7th century.

name: Isieiou Oros
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B5/ inset Isieiou Oros

name: Isola del Tinetto
description: An island in the Ligurian Sea.

name: Itea
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C4 Itea

name: Itos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Itos

name: Jebel Serbal
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Jebel Serbal

name: Kabaköy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 E4 Kabaköy

name: Kafr Aqareb
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of Roman praetorium, a temple and grottoes for pagan worship, a Byzantine basilica, and a large communal reservoir. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Kalabaka
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B1 Kalabaka

name: Kalecik
description: Kalecik (formerly known as Aralesk or Lesk) is a modern village situated ca. 5 km north of Van Kalesi in southeast Turkey. In the village church, a re-used cylindrical stone (presumably a column base) with a building inscription of the Urartian king Išpuini, son of Sarduri I (ninth century BCE), was found. Furthermore, a re-used stone slab with an inscription of the Urartian king Argišti, son of Minua and grandson of Išpuini (eighth century BCE) has also been found here.

name: Kapernabou/Kafr Nabo
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient church and residential buildings. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Kastri Livadiou ancient settlement
description: An archaeological site located 4.5km west southwest of modern Dolichi in Greece's Larisa regional unit (Thessaly). Here, a proto-byzantine walled settlement with three basilicas was built partly with spolia from a temple of Herakles and the ancient city of Doliche. On this basis, the excavators have argued for identification of the archaeology with the attested name; however, the lack so far of in-situ evidence predating the 6th century CE has led other scholars to continue to identify the original Classical-Roman polis of Doliche with the archaeological site at Sarandoporo.

name: Kastro ano Diakopto
description: Abundant spolia, artifacts, and building foundations provide evidence for an ancient settlement in the area of modern Kastro ano Diakopto and the church of Ag. Athanasios on the hill above it to the west. It is probably to be identified with ancient Boura, an attested settlement of Achaia.

name: Kephalos Island Settlement
description: On the small island of modern Kephalos in the Ambracian Gulf, archaeologists discovered a settlement, a pair of Christian basilicas, and other associated structures and small finds. The site may have begun as a refuge settlement in the late fifth / early sixth century CE and seems to have continued in use, possibly with a late monastic function, into the 10th century.

name: Kherba de Sfisifa
description: A rural location now in the modern Algerian municipal territory of Djouab (Médéa province) where Gsell and his correspondents (AAA, whence BAtlas) observed Roman ruins including a large building with columns that G. speculated might have been a church.

name: Khirbet el Quneitira
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G3 Khirbet el Quneitira

name: Khirbet Jedora
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 Khirbet Jedora

name: Kirche auf der Akropolis
description: A Byzantine church located within the fortified acropolis of Aperlae.

name: Knowlton Church and Earth Works
description: A cluster of Neolithic and Bronze Age earthworks with a twelfth-century Norman church built in the center.

name: Köşkerli
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding village in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.

name: Koustilya
description: A late-antique rural church now lacking decoration located about 6km north of the modern village of Tozeur in Tunisia.

name: Kraneia?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D3 Kraneia?

name: Kraneion Early Christian Basilica
